---
title: "Dynamic Replacement Model: Fit Summary across 4p, 8p, and 8p+FE Specifications"
author: "Kaleb Javier"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry: margin=1in
    fontsize: 11pt
    linestretch: 1.15
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    keep-tex: true
    fig-cap-location: top
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{amsmath}
        \usepackage{amssymb}
        \usepackage{booktabs}
        \usepackage{float}
        \usepackage{longtable}
        \usepackage{graphicx}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo    = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.pos = "H"
)
library(data.table)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(here)
```

# Introduction {#sec-intro}

This report summarizes the in-sample fit of the dynamic structural replacement
model under three nested specifications, each estimated by Aguirregabiria-Mira
NPL on the observed UST panel ($N = 2{,}282{,}735$ tank-year observations):

1. **4-parameter** — a single $(\kappa, K, \gamma_{\text{price}}, \gamma_{\text{risk}})$
   block, pooled across wall and regime.
2. **8-parameter** — the same primitives split by wall type (SW/DW) and
   insurance regime (FF/RB): 4 cost parameters and 4 price/risk slopes.
3. **8-parameter + state fixed effects** — the 8p structural specification
   augmented with 17 state-level intercepts $\alpha_g$ that enter the
   measurement equation (not the agent's equilibrium) for the 17 control
   states; Texas is the baseline ($\alpha_{\text{TX}} \equiv 0$).

The report is organized for direct visual and statistical comparison:
@sec-params reports the three parameter tables and the FE alphas;
@sec-comparison reports the three-way information-criteria and likelihood-ratio
table; @sec-fit reports model-implied vs. empirical action shares at the
$(\text{wall} \times \text{regime} \times \text{age bin})$ cell level for each
specification, with figures produced by a single shared plotting routine to
guarantee structural parity. Per-cell numerical fit tables are in @sec-appendix.

All artifacts are produced by `Code/Dynamic_Model/04k_Fit_Report_Artifacts.R`
from saved fit objects under `Output/Estimation_Results/`. No estimation is
re-run in the production of this report.


# Parameter Estimates {#sec-params}

## 4-parameter specification {#sec-params-4p}

@tbl-theta-4p reports point estimates with AM (2002) profile-likelihood
standard errors and 95% Wald confidence intervals.

```{r tbl-theta-4p, results="asis"}
#| label: tbl-theta-4p
#| tbl-cap: "4-parameter NPL estimates: pooled $\\kappa$, $K$, $\\gamma_{\\text{price}}$, $\\gamma_{\\text{risk}}$."
cat("\\begin{center}\n\\resizebox{\\textwidth}{!}{\n")
cat(readLines(here("Output", "Tables", "04g_Theta_Table_AM_with_CIs.tex")),
    sep = "\n")
cat("\n}\n\\end{center}\n")
```

## 8-parameter specification {#sec-params-8p}

@tbl-theta-8p splits $\kappa$ and $K$ by wall type (SW/DW) and the price/risk
slopes by insurance regime (FF/RB).

```{r tbl-theta-8p, results="asis"}
#| label: tbl-theta-8p
#| tbl-cap: "8-parameter NPL estimates: $\\kappa$ and $K$ split by wall (SW/DW); $\\gamma_{\\text{price}}$ and $\\gamma_{\\text{risk}}$ split by regime (FF/RB)."
cat("\\begin{center}\n\\resizebox{\\textwidth}{!}{\n")
cat(readLines(here("Output", "Tables", "04h_Theta_Table_8param_AM_SE.tex")),
    sep = "\n")
cat("\n}\n\\end{center}\n")
```

## 8-parameter + state fixed effects {#sec-params-8pfe}

@tbl-theta-8pfe reports the 8-structural-parameter block from the joint
$(\theta, \alpha)$ estimation. The 17 state fixed effects are reported
separately in @tbl-fe-alphas.

```{r tbl-theta-8pfe, results="asis"}
#| label: tbl-theta-8pfe
#| tbl-cap: "8-parameter + FE NPL estimates: structural parameters from the joint $(\\theta, \\alpha)$ fit. The 17 state fixed effects $\\alpha_g$ are reported separately in @tbl-fe-alphas (omitted here to avoid duplication)."
lns <- readLines(here("Output", "Tables", "04i_Theta_Table_8paramFE_AM_SE.tex"))
lns <- lns[!grepl("\\\\alpha_", lns)]
cat("\\begin{center}\n\\resizebox{\\textwidth}{!}{\n")
cat(lns, sep = "\n")
cat("\n}\n\\end{center}\n")
```

### State fixed-effect coefficients {#sec-params-fe-alphas}

The state fixed effects $\alpha_g$ enter the measurement equation (the
likelihood) only — they shift the model-implied probability of *Maintain*
in state $g$ relative to the TX baseline. They do **not** enter the agent's
equilibrium value function during counterfactual re-solves, per the
"Semantic 2" convention adopted in this codebase.

```{r tbl-fe-alphas, results="asis"}
#| label: tbl-fe-alphas
#| tbl-cap: "State fixed-effect coefficients $\\hat\\alpha_g$ from the 8p+FE specification. Texas is the baseline ($\\alpha_{\\text{TX}} \\equiv 0$); standard errors in parentheses."
cat("\\begin{center}\n")
cat(readLines(here("Output", "Tables", "04k_FE_Alphas_Compact.tex")),
    sep = "\n")
cat("\n\\end{center}\n")
```

*Note on extreme estimates.* The Missouri ($\hat\alpha_{\text{MO}} = 8.18$,
SE $0.77$) and South Dakota ($\hat\alpha_{\text{SD}} = 2.27$, SE $0.08$)
coefficients are substantially larger than the other 15 control-state alphas,
which span roughly $[-1.4,\, +0.8]$. Both are statistically distinguishable
from zero and reflect near-zero observed *Exit* and *Replace* activity in those
state-year cells relative to the TX baseline; they are not numerical artifacts.


# Model Comparison {#sec-comparison}

@tbl-comparison reports log-likelihood, information criteria, and nested
likelihood-ratio tests across the three specifications. All three are fit
on the same observed sample, so the comparison is direct.

```{r tbl-comparison, results="asis"}
#| label: tbl-comparison
#| tbl-cap: "Three-way nested model comparison. AIC $= -2\\log L + 2k$; BIC $= -2\\log L + k\\log N$. Likelihood-ratio tests: 8p vs 4p ($\\Delta k = 4$), 8p+FE vs 8p ($\\Delta k = 17$). Both LR statistics are far in the tail of their reference $\\chi^2$ distributions, with $p$-values numerically zero."
cat("\\begin{center}\n")
cat(readLines(here("Output", "Tables", "04k_Model_Comparison_3way.tex")),
    sep = "\n")
cat("\n\\end{center}\n")
```

Both nested restrictions are rejected at any conventional level. AIC and
BIC agree on the ranking 4p $\succ$ 8p $\succ$ 8p+FE (lower is better),
with the BIC penalty large enough to make 8p+FE still preferred despite
the 17 additional parameters.


# Goodness of Fit: Model-Implied vs. Empirical Cell Shares {#sec-fit}

This section reports the model-implied conditional choice probability
$\hat P(\text{action} \mid \text{state})$ at the converged $\hat\theta$
alongside the empirical action share in each $(\text{wall} \times \text{regime}
\times \text{age bin})$ cell. Empirical shares are computed by direct cell
aggregation on the observed panel. Model-implied shares are read from the
NPL-converged $\hat P$ matrix saved in each fit object. For the 8p+FE
specification, $\hat P$ is the alpha-augmented, $g$-marginalized measurement
probability (the same object the NPL outer loop uses for its CCP update).

Each model has two figures (one per wall type), each with three facets
(Maintain / Exit / Replace) and two colored lines (FF / RB regime). All six
figures are produced by a single shared plotting routine so visual
comparability across specifications is structural.

## 4-parameter fit {#sec-fit-4p}

```{r fig-fit-4p-sw}
#| label: fig-fit-4p-sw
#| fig-cap: "4-parameter fit, Single-Walled tanks. Lines are model-implied $\\hat P(\\text{action} \\mid \\text{state})$; open circles are empirical cell shares with area proportional to cell sample size."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 2.8
knitr::include_graphics(here("Output", "Figures", "04k_Fit_4param_SW.png"))
```

```{r fig-fit-4p-dw}
#| label: fig-fit-4p-dw
#| fig-cap: "4-parameter fit, Double-Walled tanks. Same construction as @fig-fit-4p-sw."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 2.8
knitr::include_graphics(here("Output", "Figures", "04k_Fit_4param_DW.png"))
```

```{r tbl-fitq-4p}
#| label: tbl-fitq-4p
#| tbl-cap: "4-parameter weighted-RMSE summary of model-vs-empirical residuals by wall, regime, and action. Residual $=$ empirical $-$ model share; weights are cell sample size."
fq4 <- fread(here("Output", "Tables", "04g_Fit_Quality_byWallRegimeAction.csv"))
kable(fq4, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE, digits = 5,
      align = c("l","l","l", rep("r", ncol(fq4) - 3L))) |>
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("scale_down"), font_size = 9)
```

## 8-parameter fit {#sec-fit-8p}

```{r fig-fit-8p-sw}
#| label: fig-fit-8p-sw
#| fig-cap: "8-parameter fit, Single-Walled tanks. Same construction as @fig-fit-4p-sw."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 2.8
knitr::include_graphics(here("Output", "Figures", "04k_Fit_8param_SW.png"))
```

```{r fig-fit-8p-dw}
#| label: fig-fit-8p-dw
#| fig-cap: "8-parameter fit, Double-Walled tanks. Same construction as @fig-fit-4p-sw."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 2.8
knitr::include_graphics(here("Output", "Figures", "04k_Fit_8param_DW.png"))
```

```{r tbl-fitq-8p}
#| label: tbl-fitq-8p
#| tbl-cap: "8-parameter weighted-RMSE summary by wall, regime, and action. Same construction as @tbl-fitq-4p."
fq8 <- fread(here("Output", "Tables", "04h_8param_FitQuality_byWallRegimeAction.csv"))
kable(fq8, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE, digits = 5,
      align = c("l","l","l", rep("r", ncol(fq8) - 3L))) |>
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("scale_down"), font_size = 9)
```

## 8-parameter + FE fit {#sec-fit-8pfe}

```{r fig-fit-8pfe-sw}
#| label: fig-fit-8pfe-sw
#| fig-cap: "8-parameter + FE fit, Single-Walled tanks. Model-implied probabilities marginalize the per-$g$ choice probability over the empirical state composition $w_g(s)$. Same plot construction as @fig-fit-4p-sw."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 2.8
knitr::include_graphics(here("Output", "Figures", "04k_Fit_8paramFE_SW.png"))
```

```{r fig-fit-8pfe-dw}
#| label: fig-fit-8pfe-dw
#| fig-cap: "8-parameter + FE fit, Double-Walled tanks. Same construction as @fig-fit-8pfe-sw."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 2.8
knitr::include_graphics(here("Output", "Figures", "04k_Fit_8paramFE_DW.png"))
```

```{r tbl-fitq-8pfe}
#| label: tbl-fitq-8pfe
#| tbl-cap: "8-parameter + FE weighted-RMSE summary by wall, regime, and action. Same construction as @tbl-fitq-4p."
fqfe <- fread(here("Output", "Tables", "04k_FitQuality_8paramFE_byWallRegimeAction.csv"))
kable(fqfe, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE, digits = 5,
      align = c("l","l","l", rep("r", ncol(fqfe) - 3L))) |>
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("scale_down"), font_size = 9)
```


# Appendix: Per-cell fit numbers {#sec-appendix}

The following three tables report the full 32-row cell-level fit numbers
for each specification. Columns: `Reg` (regime: FF/RB), `Wall` (SW/DW),
`Age` (5-year bin), `N` (cell sample size), and for each of the three
actions $a \in \{M, E, R\}$, `mod_a` $= \hat P_a(s)$ (model-implied),
`emp_a` (empirical share), and `res_a` $=$ `emp_a` $-$ `mod_a` (residual).

```{r appendix-prep, include=FALSE}
.prep_appendix <- function(path) {
  dt <- fread(path)
  dt <- dt[, .(regime, wall, age_label, n_cell,
               model_maintain, emp_maintain, res_maintain,
               model_exit,     emp_exit,     res_exit,
               model_replace,  emp_replace,  res_replace)]
  setnames(dt,
    c("regime","wall","age_label","n_cell",
      "model_maintain","emp_maintain","res_maintain",
      "model_exit",    "emp_exit",    "res_exit",
      "model_replace", "emp_replace", "res_replace"),
    c("Reg","Wall","Age","N",
      "mod_M","emp_M","res_M",
      "mod_E","emp_E","res_E",
      "mod_R","emp_R","res_R"))
  dt
}
.render_appendix <- function(dt) {
  kable(dt, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE, longtable = TRUE,
        digits = 4,
        align = c("l","l","l","r", rep("r", 9))) |>
    kable_styling(latex_options = c("repeat_header"), font_size = 8)
}
```

## 4-parameter {#sec-appendix-4p}

```{r tbl-appendix-4p}
#| label: tbl-appendix-4p
#| tbl-cap: "4-parameter per-cell fit: model-implied probability, empirical share, and residual by state cell."
.render_appendix(.prep_appendix(here("Output", "Tables", "04g_Per_Cell_Fit_Numbers_Wide.csv")))
```

## 8-parameter {#sec-appendix-8p}

```{r tbl-appendix-8p}
#| label: tbl-appendix-8p
#| tbl-cap: "8-parameter per-cell fit. Same construction as @tbl-appendix-4p."
.render_appendix(.prep_appendix(here("Output", "Tables", "04h_8param_PerCell_Fit_Wide.csv")))
```

## 8-parameter + FE {#sec-appendix-8pfe}

```{r tbl-appendix-8pfe}
#| label: tbl-appendix-8pfe
#| tbl-cap: "8-parameter + FE per-cell fit. Same construction as @tbl-appendix-4p."
.render_appendix(.prep_appendix(here("Output", "Tables", "04k_PerCell_Fit_8paramFE_Wide.csv")))
```
